properties.name: Granary Penthouse · CoWork + Gym
properties.description: A modern, light-filled penthouse in the heart of SLC's Granary District — the city's most walkable, creative neighborhood. King bed, sleeper sofa, and an in-building co-working space and gym that's genuinely excellent. Walk to TRAX, dinner, coffee, and everything downtown without a car. Thirty minutes to the best powder skiing on earth.

reviews.name: Heather
reviews.text: Sparkling clean, comfortable and spacious — complete with every amenity I could imagine. The quirky art elevated the experience for me. We will always check if this unit is available when we return to SLC.

reviews.name: Ayanga
reviews.text: Stayed here for a full month and it was perfect. Having access to the co-working space was a huge plus — great setup for focused work with 24-hour availability. Very responsive host.

properties.neighborhood_desc: The Granary District is SLC's creative hub — breweries, galleries, coffee shops and restaurants all within a few blocks. TRAX light rail is a 5-minute walk, putting downtown and the airport both under 15 minutes.

properties.name: Granary King Suite · CoWork + Gym
properties.description: A refined king suite in the Granary District — same building co-working and gym setup, fresh modern finishes, and the kind of quiet that lets you actually sleep. Ideal for work trips, ski weekends, or a downtown SLC base for two couples. Blocks from the best restaurants in the city.

reviews.name: Jane
reviews.text: Trader Joe's and coffee shops within walking distance. The place was pristine — plenty of towels, extra bedding, a kitchen that was genuinely well equipped. I would definitely stay here again.

reviews.name: Roselynn
reviews.text: My accommodation was exactly what I needed. The room was clean and the bed was nice to sleep in. Quick host response; complimentary morning coffee was a thoughtful touch.

properties.neighborhood_desc: The Granary District is SLC's creative hub — breweries, galleries, coffee shops and restaurants all within a few blocks. TRAX light rail is a 5-minute walk, putting downtown and the airport both under 15 minutes.

properties.name: Granary Studio · CoWork + Gym
properties.description: The most affordable way into the Granary building — same co-working space, same gym, same walkable neighborhood, slightly more efficient footprint. Perfect for a solo traveler, a couple, or anyone who'd rather spend the money on dinner than square footage.

reviews.name: Mary
reviews.text: Great walkable neighborhood — there's a coffee and breakfast taco shop two doors down. We cooked several nights and had everything we needed. The host's list of area suggestions was a really thoughtful touch.

properties.neighborhood_desc: The Granary District is SLC's creative hub — breweries, galleries, coffee shops and restaurants all within a few blocks. TRAX light rail is a 5-minute walk, putting downtown and the airport both under 15 minutes.

properties.name: Downtown Townhouse · 2 King Suites + Garage
properties.description: Two full king suites, 2.5 baths, and a private garage — ideal for two couples or a family who want their own space without sharing walls. A full townhouse in the middle of downtown SLC, within walking distance of the best restaurants in the city. This is the one guests rebook year after year.

reviews.name: James
reviews.text: Best place we've found in SLC — full townhouse, two real king suites, and a private garage. We were a group of four and honestly felt like we had too much space. Rebooked before we even checked out.

reviews.name: Sarah
reviews.text: Perfect for our group of four. Two full king suites meant everyone had real privacy. The garage was clutch in winter. We'd definitely be back.

properties.neighborhood_desc: Steps from Temple Square, the Gateway, and dozens of the best restaurants in SLC. Everything walkable. The private garage means you don't have to think about parking — ever.

properties.name: Downtown Townhome · 2 King Suites + 2-Car Garage
properties.description: Two king suites and a two-car garage — slightly larger than the townhouse next door, with Moroccan-inspired design details that make it genuinely beautiful. Great for a bigger group or anyone who wants space, character, and a proper downtown SLC base.

reviews.name: David
reviews.text: The two-car garage alone made this worth it for our ski trip. Beautiful space, very well stocked, and the host was incredibly responsive. Easily one of the best Airbnbs we've stayed at.

properties.neighborhood_desc: Downtown SLC — walk to Temple Square, City Creek, excellent restaurants, and TRAX. The 2-car garage makes ski days seamless: load up and go without the morning scramble.

properties.name: Exposed Brick Studio · Mountain Views
properties.description: Exposed brick, high ceilings, and genuine Wasatch mountain views from the window. A beautifully done king studio in the middle of downtown SLC — walk to everything, great light, and that rare downtown-apartment feeling that's hard to find in a short-term rental.

reviews.name: Chris
reviews.text: Waking up to mountain views in the morning was worth the whole trip. Clean, well-stocked, and genuinely comfortable. The exposed brick gives it a character most hotel rooms can't touch.

properties.neighborhood_desc: Central downtown SLC — walk to restaurants, bars, galleries, and the Eccles Theater. The Wasatch mountains are literally visible from your window, and the trailheads are 20 minutes away.

properties.name: Sugarhood Penthouse · 3BR + CoWork + Garage
properties.description: The largest property in our SLC portfolio — three full bedrooms, two baths, and a private garage. Sugarhood is the city's most charming residential neighborhood, walkable to some of the best restaurants in Utah. This one sleeps seven and still feels spacious.

reviews.name: Lauren
reviews.text: We had 6 adults and everyone had their own space. The rooftop terrace was incredible for sunset drinks. Sugarhood is the perfect neighborhood — we walked to dinner every night. Absolutely will be back.

properties.neighborhood_desc: Sugarhood — SLC's most beloved residential neighborhood. Independent restaurants, local coffee shops, and genuine community character. Sugar House Park is a 10-minute walk. Downtown is a 5-minute drive.

properties.name: ModernWest Townhome · 3BR + Garage
properties.description: A three-story new-build townhome in the up-and-coming ModernWest neighborhood — three bedrooms each with their own ensuite bath, a rooftop deck with mountain views, and a private garage. Architecturally the most interesting property in our portfolio. Ideal for a group that doesn't want to share bathrooms.

reviews.name: Marcus
reviews.text: Stunning property. We had three couples and everyone had a private ensuite — that alone makes this worth every penny. The rooftop was phenomenal for watching the mountains turn pink at sunset.

properties.neighborhood_desc: ModernWest is one of SLC's newest and fastest-growing neighborhoods — thoughtfully designed, transit-accessible, and a 10-minute drive to downtown. The 9th and 9th district restaurants are nearby.

properties.name: 9line Loft · King + Gym
properties.description: A clean, contemporary loft in the 9line district — SLC's newest transit-oriented neighborhood. In-building gym, fast WiFi, and honest SLC prices. The Jordan River Parkway is a block away for morning runs. This is where the smart traveler stays when they'd rather spend their money on ski passes.

reviews.name: Tyler
reviews.text: Exactly what you need for a ski trip — clean, comfortable, and well located. The gym was a nice bonus after long days on the mountain. Solid value.

properties.neighborhood_desc: 9line is SLC's newest transit-oriented district — developed along a reclaimed rail corridor with modern residential buildings, parks, and easy light-rail access to downtown and the airport.

properties.name: Lumber Baron's Mansion · 2 King Suites
properties.description: A two-story Victorian residence in Detroit's Brush Park — 2,000+ sq ft of restored mansion with ornate crown molding, statement chandeliers, two full king suites, and a private gym. The LCA is literally across the street. Bar Pigalle is steps away. This is the kind of place that makes you want to stay another night.

reviews.name: Sheryl
reviews.text: So close to the LCA — very walkable to everything on Woodward. We walked around with our family all day and then watched the Red Wings literally across the street. Pristine, well-appointed. Our only regret was having just one night.

reviews.name: Dalton
reviews.text: 10/10! Would definitely stay again if in town for a game. Super walkable to all the sporting venues. The mansion itself is stunning — feels like staying somewhere that actually has history.

reviews.name: Charmaine
reviews.text: Great stay in this beautiful renovated mansion. Great restaurant just steps away (Bar Pigalle). Beautiful space with real character. Loved every minute.

properties.neighborhood_desc: Brush Park is Detroit's most storied neighborhood — restored Victorian mansions, bold new construction, and the LCA literally across the street. Walk to Red Wings and Pistons games, Eastern Market, and the best of Woodward Ave.

properties.name: Lumber Baron's Penthouse · 3BR + Garage
properties.description: The top-floor penthouse of Detroit's Lumber Baron's Mansion — 2,500 sq ft with skyline views, three bedrooms (two kings, one queen), 2.5 baths, and a private garage. Ornate crown molding, statement chandeliers, curated art, and a gourmet kitchen that actually works. The finest short-term rental in Brush Park.

reviews.name: Hayley
reviews.text: Stunning — an honor to stay here. Doesn't get any better for a concert. Very walkable and you get a garage to park your car. The place is beautiful and secure. Love how everything is provided. Was a proper getaway.

reviews.name: Paul
reviews.text: Great place, comfortable with thoughtful touches throughout. Excellent neighbourhood — good coffee around the corner, a great brunch just next door. Host was very responsive. The penthouse views are something else.

properties.neighborhood_desc: Brush Park is Detroit's most storied neighborhood — restored Victorian mansions, bold new construction, and the LCA literally across the street. Walk to Red Wings and Pistons games, Eastern Market, and the best of Woodward Ave.

properties.name: Granary Queen Suite · CoWork + Gym
properties.description: A spacious queen suite in the heart of the Granary District — same in-building co-working and gym as our other Granary properties, plus an indoor fireplace for winter nights. Queen bed, sleeper sofa, full kitchen, and a genuinely walkable neighborhood that makes renting a car feel optional.

reviews.name: Kelsie
reviews.text: Perfect Granary base — everything walkable, co-working space is genuinely excellent, and the fireplace made the place feel like home. We'll be back.

properties.neighborhood_desc: The Granary District is SLC's creative hub — breweries, galleries, coffee shops and restaurants all within a few blocks. TRAX light rail is a 5-minute walk, putting downtown and the airport both under 15 minutes.

properties.name: Granary King Loft · CoWork + Gym
properties.description: Another strong Granary building entry — king bed, sleeper sofa, indoor fireplace, and the 24/7 co-working and gym that makes this building one of the best work-from-SLC setups in the city. TRAX puts you downtown or at the airport in under 15 minutes, and ski resorts are 30 minutes the other direction.

reviews.name: Devon
reviews.text: Stayed for a week on a work trip. The co-working setup is the best I've found in any Airbnb — 24 hours, fast WiFi, real desks. Then the ski resorts are 30 minutes away for the weekend.

properties.neighborhood_desc: The Granary District is SLC's creative hub — breweries, galleries, coffee shops and restaurants all within a few blocks. TRAX light rail is a 5-minute walk, putting downtown and the airport both under 15 minutes.

properties.name: Granary Twin Suites · 2 Queens + Garage
properties.description: The only two-bedroom option in our Granary building — two private queen suites each with their own ensuite bath, plus a half bath and private garage. Clean modern design, dedicated workspace, and the walkable Granary neighborhood right outside. Good for two couples who want their own space.

reviews.name: Brittany
reviews.text: Came with another couple — two full ensuite bedrooms was exactly what we needed. Beautifully done, great kitchen, garage is a bonus. Granary is the best neighborhood in SLC to be based in.

properties.neighborhood_desc: The Granary District is SLC's creative hub — breweries, galleries, coffee shops and restaurants all within a few blocks. TRAX light rail is a 5-minute walk, putting downtown and the airport both under 15 minutes.

properties.name: Downtown Loft · King + Queen + Garage
properties.description: Two private suites — one king, one queen — plus a sleeper sofa, 2.5 baths, and a private garage in the heart of downtown SLC. The layout works perfectly for mixed groups: king for one couple, queen for another, sofa for whoever drew the short straw. Walk to 900 South dining; TRAX is a block and a half away.
amenities: King suite + Queen suite + Sleeper sofa

reviews.name: Tom
reviews.text: Came with three others and the layout was ideal — king and queen suites felt like separate hotel rooms. Garage parking in downtown SLC is hard to find. Would stay again without question.

properties.neighborhood_desc: Steps from Temple Square, the Gateway, and dozens of the best restaurants in SLC. Everything walkable. The private garage means you don't have to think about parking — ever.

properties.name: Downtown Townhome · 2 Queen Suites + Garage
properties.description: Two queen ensuite suites, 2.5 baths, and a private garage in the 900 South dining district — the right pick for two couples who each want their own room and bathroom without doubling up on beds. TRAX less than two blocks away. Everything in downtown SLC is within a 10-minute walk.

reviews.name: Alicia
reviews.text: Beautifully done — two real queen beds, their own baths, great kitchen. We were here for a ski trip and the garage was the clutch detail. Everything felt thoughtfully set up.

properties.neighborhood_desc: Steps from Temple Square, the Gateway, and dozens of the best restaurants in SLC. Everything walkable. The private garage means you don't have to think about parking — ever.

properties.name: Downtown Studio · Gym + Hot Tub
properties.description: The only property in our portfolio with both a gym and a hot tub — a queen bed and sleeper sofa in central downtown, with access to the building's fitness center and hot tub on the second floor. Walking distance to City Creek, the Salt Palace, and everything downtown. Optional garage parking at $20/night.

reviews.name: Rachel
reviews.text: The hot tub after a long day of skiing was exactly what we needed. Great central location, clean and well-stocked, easy check-in. Will definitely be back.

properties.neighborhood_desc: Central downtown SLC — walk to City Creek, Temple Square, the Salt Palace convention center, and TRAX light rail. Everything downtown is within a 10-minute radius.

properties.name: Downtown Townhome · 2 Kings + Garage
properties.description: Two king ensuite bedrooms, a sleeper sofa, 2.5 baths, and a private garage in downtown SLC. Open living and kitchen layout, curated design details, easy garage entry. The pick for two couples who refuse to compromise on bed size. Walk to 900 South's best restaurants; TRAX puts you anywhere in the city.

reviews.name: Nicole
reviews.text: Came for a ski weekend with another couple. Two king beds — no negotiating. Great space, great neighborhood, easy parking in the garage. No notes.

properties.neighborhood_desc: Steps from Temple Square, the Gateway, and dozens of the best restaurants in SLC. Everything walkable. The private garage means you don't have to think about parking — ever.

properties.name: Downtown Studio · Mountain Views + Gym
properties.description: A clean, well-priced downtown studio with genuine Wasatch mountain views and in-building gym access. Queen bed and sofa, full kitchen, dedicated workspace. The smart pick for a solo traveler or couple who'd rather spend the budget on ski passes and dinners than extra square footage.

reviews.name: Greg
reviews.text: Exactly what I needed for a solo ski trip — clean, comfortable, great gym, and mountain views from the room. Smart pricing for downtown SLC.

properties.neighborhood_desc: Central downtown SLC — walk to restaurants, bars, galleries, and the Eccles Theater. The Wasatch mountains are visible from your window, and the trailheads are 20 minutes away.

properties.name: Downtown Townhome · 2 Kings + Sofa
properties.description: Two private king suites, a sofa bed, 2.5 baths, and garage parking in downtown SLC. Stylish modern townhome, open living layout, walkable to 900 South's restaurants and bars. The kind of place that photographs well and sleeps even better.

reviews.name: Jordan
reviews.text: Stylish place — both bedrooms felt like proper suites, kitchen was well stocked, and the neighborhood has great restaurants we walked to every night. Garage made ski days seamless.

properties.neighborhood_desc: Steps from Temple Square, the Gateway, and dozens of the best restaurants in SLC. Everything walkable. The private garage means you don't have to think about parking — ever.

properties.name: Downtown Townhome · King + Queen + Garage
properties.description: A king suite and a queen suite, plus a sleeper sofa, 2.5 baths, and private garage in the Granary District. Curated design, open layout, TRAX less than two blocks away. The right mix for groups where not everyone needs a king bed but everyone needs their own room.

reviews.name: Amanda
reviews.text: Two suites so both couples had real privacy, garage parking was huge for a ski trip, and the Granary neighborhood is genuinely one of the coolest places to stay in SLC.

properties.neighborhood_desc: Steps from Temple Square, the Gateway, and dozens of the best restaurants in SLC. Everything walkable. The private garage means you don't have to think about parking — ever.

properties.name: Downtown Penthouse · City + Mountain Views
properties.description: Top-floor penthouse in downtown SLC with sweeping city and Wasatch mountain views — queen bed, sleeper sofa, gym access, walking distance to Temple Square, City Creek, and the best restaurants on the main strip. Optional garage parking at $20/night. The views here in the morning are genuinely something.

reviews.name: Kevin
reviews.text: The city and mountain views from the penthouse made this stay. Woke up every morning to something incredible. Everything walkable, comfortable, easy check-in. Highly recommend.

properties.neighborhood_desc: Central downtown SLC — walk to City Creek, Temple Square, excellent restaurants, and TRAX. The Wasatch mountains are visible from your window, and ski resorts are 30 minutes away.

properties.name: Downtown Mid-Century · Renovated Brick
properties.description: A renovated historic building in downtown SLC with mid-century modern interiors and real architectural character — exposed brick, high ceilings, and details you don't find in new builds. Queen bed and pullout sofa, in-unit washer/dryer combo, full kitchen, 24/7 keypad entry. Optional garage parking at $20/night.

reviews.name: Stephanie
reviews.text: Love the character in this place — exposed brick, original details, great renovation. A real apartment feel rather than a generic STR. Perfect base for downtown SLC.

properties.neighborhood_desc: Central downtown SLC — walk to restaurants, bars, galleries, and the Eccles Theater. The Wasatch mountains are 20 minutes to trailheads, and TRAX light rail is steps away.

properties.name: Sugarhood East · 3BR + CoWork + Garage
properties.description: A three-bedroom Sugarhood apartment that sleeps seven — two kings, a queen, and a queen sleeper sofa. Free garage parking, in-building co-working and gym, on the TRAX light rail line, close to Sugar House dining and nightlife. And yes, we take your dog.

reviews.name: Tara
reviews.text: Came with a big group — 6 adults and our dog — and the space handled it easily. Three real bedrooms, great garage parking, Sugar House is a fantastic neighborhood. Co-working setup is a nice bonus.

properties.neighborhood_desc: Sugarhood — SLC's most beloved residential neighborhood. Independent restaurants, local coffee shops, and genuine community character. Sugar House Park is a 10-minute walk. Downtown is a 5-minute drive.

properties.name: 9line Loft II · King + Gym
properties.description: The second loft in our 9line building — king bed, sofa, in-building gym, and honest SLC prices. The 9line district sits along a reclaimed rail corridor with modern buildings and parks. Jordan River Parkway is a block away for morning runs. Great TRAX access to downtown and the airport.

reviews.name: Ben
reviews.text: Clean, comfortable, well priced. The gym is a nice perk after ski days. Good transit access — downtown in 15 minutes without a car. Solid option for a ski trip on a budget.

properties.neighborhood_desc: 9line is SLC's newest transit-oriented district — developed along a reclaimed rail corridor with modern residential buildings, parks, and easy light-rail access to downtown and the airport.
